TT.-5(485)/99-TJ/T. 7TT.J 42 til/2000 ( I ) -- 1 of 2 -- THE GAZETTE OF INDIA EXTRAORDINARY [PART IIâ€”SEC 3(0] MINISTRY OF LAW, JUSTICE AND COMPANY AFFAIRS (Department of Legal Affairs) NOTIFICATION New Delhi, the 5th January, 2000 G.S.R. 17(E).â€”In exercise of the powers conferred by Section 15 of the Notaries Act, 1952 (53 of 1952), the Central Government hereby makes the following rules further lo amend the Notaries Rules, 1956, namely â€” (1) These rules may be called the Notaries (Amendment) Rules, 2000. (ii) This shall come into force from the date of the publication in the Official Gazette. 2. Jn the Notaries Rules, 1956, in rule 3, sub-rule (a), the following shall be substituted, namely .â€” "(a) a person had been practising at least for ten years, or (aa) a person belonging to Scheduled Castes/Scheduled Tnbes and other backward classes had been practising at least for seven years, or (ab) a woman who had been practising at least for seven years, as a legal pactitioner, or" [No. 5(485)/99-NC] KRISHNA KUMAR, Jt Secy & Legal Adviser Punted liv the Manager, (iovl ol India Press Ring Road Maj.ipun, New Delhi-1 KJIIM ,md Published hv ihe Conlrullei o!
